text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town nestling among mountains high.

There lived a bird watcher. Every morning he would scout those mountain slopes for vibrant feathered friends.

One morning Gerald noticed something most unusual—a golden bird fluttering around the tall oaks.

He watched. And watch.

That bird could talk!

That bird was him!

With twinkling blue eyes?

Oh my stars!

A feather boa?

Yes! How charming!

He decided to learn its song.

So they sang.

They sang all day long.

There was laughter like bells!

Evening stars.

That golden bird was his pet.

He’d talk rabbit.

Just Gerald?

Why yes?

Just Gerald.

One afternoon.

That golden bird blew him kisses.

With talons.

That’s cheeky!

Perhaps just affection?

His feathers rustled.

How curious.

They danced everywhere!

That afternoon!

Just Gerald.

That's my favorite.

Such loyalty.

One could learn.

Just listen.

And listen good.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
